acceptanceCriteria: A new versioned rigor.test-integrity-event.v1 artifact matching the shape specified in docs/test-integrity.md is written append-only under the task's evidence directory with mode shadow and enforcement none, and contains only bounded counts, enumerated strings, repository-relative paths, and opaque digests - never raw command output, matched source text, or test source

Exactly the five shadow-candidate signals selected by the #21 catalog are implemented as deterministic diff-based detectors: TI-05 skip/only/todo markers added, TI-06 test-path file deleted without a rename pair, TI-07 net assertion-token decline, TI-08 snapshot churn alongside implementation changes, TI-09 verification-adjacent config or script changed; each records label advisory-interpretation and computation deterministic with a named detector version

Events link taskId, contract-base baseSha, headSha or explicit null with a worktree digest when dirty, changed test paths, and optional attemptArtifactId and verificationArtifactId that are explicitly null when absent

Shadow observations cannot change verification status, attempt progress, review, release-check, or CI gate behavior: no existing enforcement code path reads the new artifacts, existing check and mutation controls remain byte-for-byte unchanged in behavior, and all pre-existing tests pass unmodified

A human classification artifact records per-signal true-positive/false-positive/uncertain verdicts as a labeled human-reported declaration that never upgrades to a verified fact, and a deterministic guard prevents classifying an event of a task whose attempt is still unfinished, so a delegated model cannot confirm its own observation mid-attempt

rigor retrospect reports per-signal fired counts with explicit denominators, and reports unreviewed observations separately from human-classified true/false-positive counts, never hiding missing data behind a bare total

Synthetic fixtures exercise, for each implemented signal, at least one must-fire true positive and one must-not-fire plausible false positive from the catalog's falsifiable-tests table (including the TI-06 rename pairing and TI-05 non-marker cases), plus schema/version compatibility tests for the new artifacts

docs/test-integrity.md status moves the five signals from proposed to shadow-implemented without weakening the what-must-never-be-advertised section, and orchestration/architecture docs describe the scan and classification commands and the retrospect extension

The recorded required check git diff --check passes; npm run test:all, npm run bench, and claude plugin validate . --strict are rerun by the orchestrator and their results reported in the pull request
dist/rigor.cjs is rebuilt from the final source and committed
constraints: implement only issue #22 scope: shadow collection of the five selected signals, the classification artifact, and the retrospect extension; no enforcement, no CI gate change, no promotion rule, no threshold
do not modify .rigor/policy.json, .rigor/rigor-ci.cjs, .github/workflows/**, or any path outside allowedPaths
signal detectors use built-in documented defaults for test-path globs and token lists (recorded as detector name/version); no policy schema change and no per-repository configuration in this task
do not implement TI-10 through TI-16, coverage or mutation adapters, or any framework-specific analyzer beyond the catalog's five selected diff-token/path signals
do not weaken, skip, or delete existing tests; existing enforcement semantics (fingerprints, weakeningSignals, progress, policy protection) must remain unchanged
shadow events and classifications are advisory evidence: never claim they detect intent, prove weakening, or make tests sufficient; keep the catalog's prohibited-claims section intact
no GitHub write beyond pushing the task branch and opening the pull request; merge requires the human-approval path recorded in the preflight
stopConditions: a required check fails twice without a new hypothesis
